Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Simple This scenario outlines a simplified cyber attack leveraging the SolarWinds compromise model against the Energy sector. The attackers, with expert skills, aim to gain initial access through supply chain compromise, execute their payload via user interaction, and cause impact by manipulating critical data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Trusted Vendor Systems, Operational Technology
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Software Update Channels, Phishing, Command Line Interface

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 439; Name: Manipulation During Distribution
stage.description: The attackers compromise a trusted vendor's software update process, similar to the SolarWinds incident, gaining initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 663; Name: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: Once inside, the attackers inject malicious code into legitimate software updates, which users unknowingly execute.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 153; Name: Input Data Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers alter critical operational data within the energy sector to disrupt services, causing significant impact.

Scenario.summary: The SolarWinds-inspired attack on the Energy sector involves compromising trusted vendors, executing malicious payloads via user interaction, and impacting operations by manipulating critical data. The attackers leverage expert-level skills to remain undetected while achieving their objectives.
